**Trajectory assessment:** Monday 2026-04-27 was a **high-throughput execution day** — the inverse of 2026-04-26's high-architectural Sunday. The dominant signal was the lit-search pipeline draining the 5-day backlog, processing 58 items, producing the project's first-ever two-INCORPORATE day (PREMISE-012, PREMISE-013), and articulating its own operational principles for the first time (ASSUMPTION-072, 073, 074 — symmetric to the BRIEFING-LAYER-EPISTEMIC-COMMITMENTS cluster's emergence on 2026-04-20). The cost paid for that throughput is captured in today's 12-to-8 presumption-to-assumption ratio (highest observed in project history): the lit-search-layer's stated commitments embedded multiple unstated premises (PRESUMPTION-081, 082, 085, 086), and the design-project session's authorial reframing (ASSUMPTION-076) surfaced a critical recursive-specialist-reading risk (PRESUMPTION-089 joining the CRITICAL/HIGH self-awareness cluster with PRESUMPTION-002 and 024). Infrastructure took a new hit: today's two browser-auth failures plus two new pre-flight-stalls (cumulative 6 in 2 days) extend the sandbox-infrastructure cluster to 5 channels, the strongest case yet for the combined OPEN-039 escalation track. **If any one action moves the pipeline forward most**, it is escalating the 5-channel sandbox-infrastructure cluster as a single track to Anthropic — git-lock + mount-topology + egress + browser-auth + pre-flight-grant jointly block Phase 6, the 1pm interactive sessions, the daily Chat↔Cowork sync, and the candidate DECISION-026. **If a second**, it is propagating ASSUMPTION-076 (PRS authorial reframing) into the C2A2 wiki's per-tradition `prs_triplets.md` files and reviewing PRESUMPTION-089's recursive-specialist-reading risk against yesterday's specialist outputs. **If a third**, it is drafting candidate DECISION-026 (specialist pre-flight directory-grant timeout) — 6 cumulative stalls in 2 days is sufficient empirical signal to ship a minimal-form candidate (per the ASSUMPTION-062 weak-circuit-breaker > none principle).
